ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general yogurt class (FOODON_03301494). No specific Xinjiang handmade yogurt entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers plain yoghurt. Xinjiang handmade variant is a regional preparation; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226760005 is the general yogurt substance concept; no Xinjiang-specific entry exists.

main_ingredients: live bacterial cultures (Lactobacillus bulgaricus, Streptococcus thermophilus)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Xinjiang handmade yogurt is approximately 150 g (one small bowl or cup), as commonly served in Xinjiang-style restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Yogurt, plain, whole milk — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 171284)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Xinjiang handmade yogurt is a set-style whole-milk yogurt fermented with traditional cultures. No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific regional product. USDA SR Legacy plain whole-milk yogurt (FDC ID 171284) is the closest approved analogue. Values are applied as-is; the handmade preparation method does not substantially alter the macronutrient profile relative to standard whole-milk plain yogurt.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for yogurt, plain, full-fat
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 36 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain full-fat yogurt (mean GI ~36, low category). Xinjiang handmade yogurt is a plain whole-milk set yogurt with no added sugar; the GI is expected to be equivalent to standard plain full-fat yogurt. GL per serving = (36 × 4.7 × 1.5) / 100 = 2.5 (rounded to 1 decimal place), based on 150 g serving and 4.7 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (46 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Xinjiang handmade yogurt contains only 46 mg sodium per 100 g, making it a low-sodium dairy option suitable for individuals managing hypertension, provided no salt is added during preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 36) and moderate carbohydrate content (4.7 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 36 and a glycemic load of approximately 2.5 per 150 g serving, Xinjiang handmade yogurt produces a minimal postprandial glucose response and can be included in a Type 2 Diabetes meal plan in moderate portions, particularly as a protein- and calcium-rich snack.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (2.1 g per 100 g) from whole milk; beneficial probiotic cultures
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Xinjiang handmade yogurt contains moderate saturated fat from whole milk (2.1 g per 100 g); individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume it in reasonable portions, and may consider low-fat yogurt alternatives if saturated fat intake is a clinical concern, while benefiting from its probiotic content which may support cardiometabolic health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Xinjiang handmade yogurt (新疆手工酸奶, Xīnjiāng shǒugōng suānnǎi) is a traditional set-style fermented dairy product originating from the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region of northwest China. It is prepared by fermenting fresh whole cow's milk with live bacterial cultures, typically Lactobacillus bulgaricus and Streptococcus thermophilus, and allowing it to set naturally without mechanical agitation, producing a thick, creamy, slightly tangy yogurt with a smooth texture. Unlike commercially produced yogurt, the handmade variety is valued for its richer flavour, denser consistency, and artisanal character. It is commonly served plain in a small bowl or clay pot, sometimes accompanied by honey, dried fruit, or nuts. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 61 kcal per 100 g, with 3.5 g protein, 3.3 g fat, and 4.7 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-energy, nutrient-dense dairy food. It is a good source of calcium (121 mg per 100 g), vitamin B12, riboflavin, and potassium. Its low glycemic index (GI ~36) and low glycemic load make it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels, including those with Type 2 Diabetes. The low sodium content (46 mg per 100 g) makes it appropriate for hypertension management. In Singapore, it is served at Xinjiang and northwest Chinese restaurants as a dessert or snack item.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Xinjiang handmade yogurt exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or USDA FoodData Central under this specific regional name. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy plain whole-milk yogurt (FDC ID 171284), which is the closest approved analogue. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain full-fat yogurt. The handmade preparation method does not substantially alter the macronutrient profile. Confidence is medium due to the use of an analogous source rather than an exact match.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Grains and staples' (as in the input seed record) to 'Dairy and eggs', which is the correct classification for yogurt. 2. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy FDC ID 171284 (plain whole-milk yogurt) as no direct Xinjiang handmade yogurt entry exists in any approved primary source. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source; yogurt contains no dietary fibre. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source for plain whole-milk yogurt. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy source for plain (non-fortified) whole-milk yogurt; this product is not vitamin D-fortified. 6. GI of 36 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain full-fat yogurt; no direct GI measurement for Xinjiang handmade yogurt was found in the approved GI literature. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 171284. 8. Halal status is assumed based on the product being plain fermented milk with no pork-derived ingredients; verification from the serving restaurant is recommended. 9. The input cuisine_tags included 'Noodle', which is not applicable to this yogurt product and has been disregarded.
